title: cyathea rumphii
intro: Cyathea rumphii, also known as the Rough Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the Cyathea genus. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cyathea rumphii include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's fiddleheads are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
